name: Not for Wimps Chocolate Custard Pudding
recipeIngredient: 2 cups 2% milk (don't use whole or cream)
4 egg yolks, with the small white "tails" removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Place cornstarch in a small measuring cup.
2. Remove 1/4 cup of milk from the 2 cups and set aside.
3. Pour the reserved milk over the cornstarch. Do not stir.
4. In an 8-cup glass pitcher, combine the remaining milk and chocolate. Microwave on high for about 3 minutes, then remove and beat with a whisk or fork until the chocolate is melted. A few flecks of chocolate may remain; this is okay.
5. Beat egg yolks with sugar until combined.
6. Gradually pour a bit of the hot chocolate/milk mixture into the beaten egg yolks, stirring constantly.
7. Continue adding more of the hot chocolate/milk mixture to the egg yolks until about 3/4 cup has been incorporated.
8. Gently stir in the smooth cornstarch mixture, which will initially appear stiff but will blend smoothly as it is heated.
9. Stir the cornstarch mixture into the chocolate-egg mixture well.
10. Return the combined mixtures to the microwave and heat on high for about 1 minute. Scrape the sides of the container well, beat the mixture, and continue heating in 1-minute intervals or less until thickened.
11. The mixture may seem to thicken quickly at the edges and may even appear to curdle. Do not panic; remove from heat, beat vigorously, and it will smooth out.
12. When the consistency is that of a light mayonnaise and appears fluffy and pudding-like, add butter and stir well. Voila!
